title: Post-Hoc Compression-Aware Differential Privacy: Optimizing DP Training for Deployed Compressed Models
description: We propose Post-Hoc Compression-Aware DP-SGD (PHCA-DP-SGD), a training algorithm that optimizes differential privacy for models that will undergo post-hoc compression. Unlike pre-pruning approaches that require public data, PHCA-DP-SGD uses continuous per-parameter clipping weights based on estimated survival probabilities to allocate privacy budget more efficiently. This enables better privacy-utility trade-offs for deployed compressed models without requiring any public data.
motivation: Model compression exacerbates privacy leakage (CompLeak, 2025), yet compression is essential for deployment. Current practice trains with DP-SGD assuming the model will be deployed as-is, then compresses as an afterthought. Pre-pruning approaches (Adamczewski et al., 2023) address this but require public data for parameter selection, which is often unavailable. No existing work studies post-hoc compression-aware DP training without public data requirements.
proposed_approach: PHCA-DP-SGD introduces three key innovations: (1) Continuous per-parameter clipping weights based on estimated survival probability under compression (not binary masks like AdaDPIGU); (2) Compression-aware noise calibration that adds less noise to parameters likely to be pruned; (3) Survival probability estimation from private training dynamics (no public data required). The algorithm targets post-hoc compression rather than training-time pruning.
hypothesis: By incorporating knowledge of target post-hoc compression into DP-SGD training without requiring public data, we can achieve better privacy-utility trade-offs than both (1) standard DP-SGD followed by post-hoc compression, and (2) pre-pruning approaches that require public data. Specifically, PHCA-DP-SGD will achieve 3-5% higher test accuracy at the same privacy budget (ε=3, δ=1e-5) and compression ratio (70% sparsity), matching or exceeding pre-pruning performance without public data.

confirm: PHCA-DP-SGD achieves statistically significantly better test accuracy (p<0.05) than standard DP-SGD + post-hoc compression at same ε and compression ratio, across 3+ datasets
PHCA-DP-SGD achieves comparable or better accuracy than Adamczewski et al. (2023) pre-pruning without using public data
PHCA-DP-SGD outperforms AdaDPIGU on post-hoc compression tasks, demonstrating value of continuous clipping weights over binary masking
Derive theoretical framework for post-hoc compression-DP interaction validated empirically
refute: Standard DP-SGD + compression achieves accuracy within 1% of PHCA-DP-SGD, suggesting no benefit from compression-awareness
PHCA-DP-SGD cannot match pre-pruning performance without public data
Binary masking matches or exceeds continuous per-parameter clipping performance

dp_training.title: Deep Learning with Differential Privacy
dp_training.difference: We extend DP-SGD to be compression-aware for post-hoc compression; they assume model deployed as-is

dp_training.title: Rényi Differential Privacy
dp_training.difference: We use RDP for privacy accounting but extend to account for compression-aware clipping

pre_pruning_dp.title: Pre-Pruning and Gradient-Dropping Improve Differentially Private Image Classification
pre_pruning_dp.difference: They use public data for pre-pruning and gradient-dropping during training; we target post-hoc compression without public data requirements

pre_pruning_dp.title: Differential Privacy Meets Neural Network Pruning
pre_pruning_dp.difference: They study training-time pruning with public data; we study post-hoc compression without public data

importance_based_methods.title: AdaDPIGU: Differentially Private SGD with Adaptive Clipping and Importance-Based Gradient Updates for Deep Neural Networks
importance_based_methods.difference: They use binary importance-based masking with progressive unfreezing; we use continuous per-parameter clipping weights based on survival probability; they target training efficiency, we target post-hoc compression optimization

importance_based_methods.title: Defending Membership Inference Attacks via Privacy-aware Sparsity Tuning
importance_based_methods.difference: They use adaptive L1 regularization for empirical privacy (no DP); we target certified DP with post-hoc compression

compression_privacy.title: CompLeak: Deep Learning Model Compression Exacerbates Privacy Leakage
compression_privacy.difference: They identify the problem (compression increases leakage); we propose a solution (compression-aware DP training)

privacy_attacks.title: Membership Inference Attacks Against Machine Learning Models
privacy_attacks.difference: We use MIA as evaluation metric; they introduced the attack

privacy_attacks.title: Membership Inference Attacks From First Principles
privacy_attacks.difference: We use their evaluation methodology; our defense is training-time

baselines: Adamczewski et al. (2023) pre-pruning (with public data)

computational_requirements.justification: Training ResNet-18 on CIFAR-10 with DP-SGD takes ~30 minutes per configuration. With 12 configurations × 3 datasets × 3 runs = 108 training runs, plus evaluation, total is approximately 6 hours.

novelty_claims: First work to target post-hoc compression (not training-time pruning) in DP training without requiring public data
Continuous per-parameter clipping weights based on survival probability, unlike binary masking in AdaDPIGU
Compression-aware noise calibration based on anticipated post-hoc compression
Survival probability estimation from private training dynamics without public data or additional privacy cost

vs_adamczewski_2023: We target post-hoc compression; they target training-time pre-pruning
We require no public data; they require public data for parameter selection
We use continuous clipping weights; they use binary gradient dropping
vs_adadpigu_2025: We target post-hoc compression optimization; they target training efficiency
We use continuous survival-probability-based clipping weights; they use binary importance-based masking
We estimate importance from training dynamics; they use DP pretraining for importance estimation
vs_past_2024: We provide certified DP guarantees; they provide only empirical privacy (MIA resistance)
We target post-hoc compression; they target training-time sparsity tuning
